title: geum sylvaticum subsp. pyrenaicum
intro: Geum sylvaticum subsp. pyrenaicum, also known as the alpine avens or European avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Geum sylvaticum subsp. pyrenaicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
